framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Every AI use case must have clear owners with defined accountability for AI governance across the organisation.
mandatory_records: AI governance policy|job descriptions|executive accountable official assignment|responsible-parties register|governance forum minutes
notes: Guidance names two roles: Executive accountable official (org-wide); Responsible parties (approve/operate/monitor each AI system)

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Identify affected groups and potential harms before deployment; plan mitigation accordingly.
mandatory_records: AI screening/intake form|impact assessment record|stakeholder-identification record
notes: Pre-deployment; covers end-users third parties public and environment

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Treat AI use like any other material business risk — identify score treat and monitor.
mandatory_records: enterprise risk register entries|risk treatment records|control records|risk review schedule
notes: Integrate with existing ERM; do not create a parallel AI-only risk process

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Maintain transparency about AI use and limitations — internally to staff and externally to end-users and stakeholders.
mandatory_records: AI system register|privacy notices|supplier AI disclosure|end-user disclosure content|model-card equivalent
notes: AI system register is the keystone artefact; external disclosure required for consequential AI

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: AI systems must be tested before use and monitored in life; incidents captured and actioned against pre-agreed thresholds.
mandatory_records: pre-deployment test results|monitoring metrics|drift-detection records|incident thresholds|incident log
notes: Incident thresholds must be defined up front not post-hoc

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: People remain responsible for decisions and outcomes — AI supports but does not replace human judgement on consequential actions.
mandatory_records: human-oversight process documentation|training records|appeals/recourse procedures|override log
notes: Approval override and appeals pathways must be documented per AI use case

control_description: Policies processes procedures and practices across the organisation related to the mapping measuring and managing of AI risks are in place transparent and implemented effectively.
Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered responsible and trained for mapping measuring and managing AI risks.

control_name: Workforce diversity equity inclusion and accessibility
control_description: Workforce diversity equity inclusion and accessibility processes are prioritised in the mapping measuring and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.
notes: DEIA grouped under accountability family as workforce accountability

control_name: Commitment to AI risk management culture
control_description: Organisational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
notes: Risk culture is a dimension of organisational accountability

control_description: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.
Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.
Context is established and understood.
Categorisation of the AI system is performed.
AI capabilities targeted usage goals and expected benefits and costs are understood.
Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system including third-party software and data.

control_name: Impacts to individuals groups and society
control_description: Impacts to individuals groups communities organisations and society are characterised.
Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics (validity reliability safety security privacy transparency accountability fairness).
Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.
Feedback about efficacy of measurement is gathered and assessed.
AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from Map and Measure are prioritised responded to and managed.
Strategies to maximise AI benefits and minimise negative impacts are planned prepared implemented documented and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are regularly monitored and risk controls are applied and documented.
Risk treatments including response and recovery and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.
mandatory_records: risk treatment plan|incident response playbook|communication plan

control_description: AI systems must operate safely securely and robustly with risks identified assessed and managed throughout the AI life cycle.
mandatory_records: safety testing records|security controls|robustness test evidence|incident response plan
notes: Cross-sectoral; interpreted by each UK sector regulator within their remit

control_description: Parties directly affected by AI should be able to access sufficient information about AI systems to scrutinise and challenge outcomes.
mandatory_records: transparency notices|explainability documentation|model cards or equivalents
notes: Proportional to risk and context not full explainability for every system

control_description: AI systems should not undermine legal rights or discriminate unfairly or create unfair commercial outcomes.
notes: Fairness as impact-on-groups; also touches risk_management (bias risk) via bridge relationship

control_description: Governance measures should ensure effective oversight of AI supply and use with clear accountability for outcomes.
Users and affected third parties should be able to contest AI decisions and seek redress where outcomes are harmful or unfair.
notes: Especially salient for consumer-facing and high-impact AI

mandatory_records: strict permission scoping|sandboxed execution|argument validation|tool invocation policy controls
notes: Over-privileged tools / poisoned tool descriptors / unvalidated shell commands

control_description: High-privilege credentials and tokens inherited by agents are unintentionally reused escalated or passed across agents.
mandatory_records: short-lived credentials|task-scoped permissions|policy-enforced authorization|isolated agent identities
notes: Cached SSH keys / cross-agent delegation / confused-deputy scenarios

control_description: Compromised tools plugins templates or external MCP servers fetched at runtime alter agent behavior or expose data.
notes: Malicious MCP servers / poisoned prompt templates / vulnerable third-party agents

control_description: Agents generate or execute code or commands unsafely including shells scripts and deserialization.
mandatory_records: treat generated code as untrusted|remove direct evaluation|hardened sandboxes|require preview before execution
notes: Generated patches run directly / prompt injection → shell / unsafe deserialization

control_description: Attackers poison memory systems embeddings RAG databases and summaries to influence future agent decisions.
mandatory_records: memory segmentation|input filtering|provenance tracking|expiry of suspicious entries
notes: RAG poisoning / cross-tenant context leakage / long-term drift from adversarial content

control_description: Multi-agent message exchanges lack authentication encryption or semantic validation enabling instruction injection.
mandatory_records: mutual TLS|signed payloads|anti-replay protections|authenticated discovery mechanisms
notes: Spoofed agent identities / replayed delegation / tampering on unprotected channels

control_description: Errors in one agent propagate across planning execution memory and downstream systems compounding rapidly.
mandatory_records: isolation boundaries|rate limits|circuit breakers|pre-deployment multi-step plan testing
notes: Hallucinating planner issuing destructive tasks / poisoned state propagated through deployment agents

control_description: Users over-trust agent recommendations allowing attackers or misaligned agents to influence decisions or extract information.
mandatory_records: forced confirmation for sensitive actions|immutable logs|risk indicators shown to user|avoid persuasive language in outputs
notes: Subtle backdoors in coding assistants / fraudulent transfer approvals / credential extraction by support agents

control_description: Compromised or misaligned agents act harmfully while appearing legitimate persisting across sessions.
notes: Continuous data exfiltration post-injection / silent approval of unsafe actions / deletion of backups

control_description: Route user text uploaded documents and retrieved content through the same input-validation and prompt-injection safeguards defined in LLM01:2025 before they can influence goal selection planning or tool calls.

control_name: Minimize impact via least privilege and human approval
control_description: Enforce least privilege for agent tools and require human approval for high-impact or goal-changing actions to contain any successful hijack.

control_name: Define and lock agent system prompts

control_name: Validate user intent and agent intent at run time

control_description: Bind the declared goal constraints and context to each execution cycle in a signed envelope restricting run-time use.

control_description: Apply content/data-rule filtering to RAG inputs emails calendar invites uploaded files external APIs browsing output and peer-agent messages using CDR prompt-carrier detection and content filtering before they can influence agent goals or actions.

control_description: Establish a behavioral baseline including goal state tool-use patterns and invariant properties; track a stable identifier for the active goal and alert on any deviations such as unexpected goal changes anomalous tool sequences or shifts from baseline.

control_name: Periodic red-team tests simulating goal override

control_description: Require explicit authentication for each tool invocation and human confirmation for high-impact or destructive actions; present a dry-run plan or diff preview before final approval.

control_description: Run tool or code execution in isolated sandboxes; enforce outbound allowlists and deny all non-approved network destinations.

control_description: Treat LLM or planner outputs as untrusted; a pre-execution Policy Enforcement Point validates intent and arguments enforces schemas and rate limits issues short-lived credentials and revokes or audits on drift.

control_description: Apply usage ceilings (cost rate or token budgets) with automatic revocation or throttling when exceeded.

control_description: Grant temporary credentials or API tokens that expire immediately after use; bind keys to specific user sessions to prevent lateral abuse.

control_description: Maintain immutable logs of all tool invocations and parameter changes; continuously monitor for anomalous execution rates unusual tool-chaining patterns and policy violations.

control_description: Issue short-lived narrowly scoped tokens per task using per-agent identities and short-lived credentials (mTLS certificates or scoped tokens) to limit blast radius and mitigate un-scoped inheritance.

control_description: Run per-session sandboxes with separated permissions and memory wiping state between tasks to prevent Memory-Based Escalation and reduce Cross-Repository Data Exfiltration.

control_description: Re-verify each privileged step with a centralized policy engine that checks external data stopping Cross-Agent Trust Exploitation and Reflection Loop Elevation.

control_description: Require human approval for high-privilege or irreversible actions as a safety net against Memory-Based Escalation Cross-Agent Trust Exploitation and Maintenance-Window attacks.

control_description: Bind OAuth tokens to a signed intent that includes subject audience purpose and session; reject any token use where the bound intent doesn't match the current request.

control_description: Evaluate platforms that integrate agents into identity and access management as managed non-human identities with scoped credentials audit trails and lifecycle controls (Microsoft Entra AWS Bedrock Agents Salesforce Agentforce Workday ASOR Google Vertex AI).

control_name: Bind permissions to subject resource purpose and duration

control_description: Monitor when an agent gains new permissions indirectly through delegation chains; flag cases where a low-privilege agent inherits or is handed higher-privilege scopes during multi-agent workflows.

control_description: Sign and attest manifests prompts and tool definitions; require and operationalize SBOMs and AIBOMs with periodic attestations; maintain inventory of AI components and use curated registries while blocking untrusted sources.

control_description: Allowlist and pin dependencies; scan for typo-squats (PyPI npm LangChain LlamaIndex); verify provenance before install or activation; auto-reject unsigned or unverified packages.

control_description: Put prompts orchestration scripts and memory schemas under version control with peer review; scan for anomalies.

control_description: Enforce mutual auth and attestation via PKI and mTLS; no open registration; sign and verify all inter-agent messages.
